title: Citrusy Shattering Festive Russian Yuzu-Kosho Grill
description: A unique fusion of Russian and Mediterranean flavors featuring yuzu-kosho, grilled to perfection in a traditional Kazan cauldron.
instructions: Preheat the Kazan cauldron to medium-high heat, about 375°F (190°C).
Season the cod fillets with kosher salt, black pepper, and black sea salt.
In a small bowl, whisk together yuzu juice, yuzu-kosho, and lemon juice. Brush the mixture onto both sides of the cod fillets.
Add the olive oil to the preheated Kazan cauldron and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Sear the cod fillets for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and cooked through.
Transfer the cod fillets to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same Kazan cauldron, add the white wine, garlic, and grated ginger. Scrape the bottom of the cauldron with a wooden spatula to release any browned bits.
Bring the wine mixture to a simmer and cook until it has reduced by half, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the unsalted butter until melted and smooth.
Add the chopped dill and chives to the wine mixture and cook for an additional minute.
To assemble the dish, place a cod fillet on each plate and spoon the citrusy yuzu-kosho sauce over the top.
Garnish with additional dill and chives, if desired.
Serve immediately, while the sauce is still warm and the cod is still moist and flaky.
